title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses due to ransom payments or downtime.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored in isolated, secure environments to prevent ransomware encryption.
Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to identify and mitigate ransomware activity in real-time.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: Unrestricted IP address binding in the AMD Device Metrics Exporter (ROCm ecosystem) could allow a...
description: Unrestricted IP address binding in the AMD Device Metrics Exporter (ROCm ecosystem) could allow a remote attacker to perform unauthorized changes to the GPU configuration, potentially resulting in loss of availability

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-0481.

title: Cross-site request forgery vulnerability exists in Musetheque V4 Information Disclosure for...
description: Cross-site request forgery vulnerability exists in Musetheque V4 Information Disclosure for IPKNOWLEDGE V4L1 rev2203.0 and earlier. If a user views a malicious page while logged-in to the affected product, unexpected operations may be done.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform unauthorized operations on the affected product if a logged-in user visits a malicious page. This can lead to data manipulation, unauthorized access, or information disclosure.
recommended_actions: Implement anti-CSRF tokens to validate requests and prevent unauthorized actions.
Advise users to avoid clicking on untrusted links while logged into the application.
Update Musetheque V4 to the latest version or apply any available patches addressing CVE-2026-28761.

title: An out of bounds read in the remote management firmware could allow a privileged attacker read a...
description: An out of bounds read in the remote management firmware could allow a privileged attacker read a limited section of memory outside of established bounds potentially resulting in loss of confidentiality or availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2024-21950.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ksmbd: validate inherited...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ksmbd: validate inherited ACE SID length

smb_inherit_dacl() walks the parent directory DACL loaded from the
security descriptor xattr. It verifies that each ACE contains the fixed
SID header before using it, but does not verify that the variable-length
SID described by sid.num_subauth is fully contained in the ACE.

A malformed inheritable ACE can advertise more subauthorities than are
present in the ACE. compare_sids() may th

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's ksmbd module could allow attackers to exploit improperly validated Access Control Entries (ACEs), potentially leading to privilege escalation or unauthorized access to sensitive data. This could compromise the integrity and confidentiality of systems relying on ksmbd for SMB file sharing.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest Linux kernel patches that address CVE-2026-43490.
Audit systems using ksmbd to ensure proper security configurations and monitor for any suspicious activity.
Implement network segmentation and restrict access to SMB services to trusted hosts only.

title: Improper isolation of VCN-JPEG HW register space could allow a malicious Guest Virtual Machine ...
description: Improper isolation of VCN-JPEG HW register space could allow a malicious Guest Virtual Machine (VM) or a process to perform unauthorized access to the register space of the JPEG cores assigned a victim VM/process, potentially gaining arbitrary read/write access to the victim VM/process data.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2024-36323.

description: Cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in Musetheque V4 Information Disclosure for IPKNOWLEDGE V4L1 rev2203.0 and earlier. If a file containing malicious contents is uploaded, an arbitrary script may be executed on a user's web browser when viewing the administration page showing the information of the file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-24662.

title: A race condition in the MxGPU-Virtualization driver’s ioctl path caused by concurrent...
description: A race condition in the MxGPU-Virtualization driver’s ioctl path caused by concurrent unsynchronized access to the global variable amdgv_cmd in an unlocked ioctl handler could be exploited by an attacker to trigger a heap-based buffer overflow, potentially resulting in denial-of-service within the vulnerable system context.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2025-52532.

title: Improper verification of cryptographic signature in the Radeon RGB tool could allow a malicious...
description: Improper verification of cryptographic signature in the Radeon RGB tool could allow a malicious file placed in the installation directory to be run with elevated privileges potentially leading to arbitrary code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Radeon RGB tool allows for improper verification of cryptographic signatures, enabling a malicious actor to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges. This could lead to system compromise, data exfiltration, or further exploitation.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update for the Radeon RGB tool as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict write permissions to the installation directory to prevent unauthorized file placement.
Monitor systems for unusual activity, especially processes running with elevated privileges, and implement application whitelisting.

title: Improper isolation of shared resources within the CPU operation cache on Zen 2-based products...
description: Improper isolation of shared resources within the CPU operation cache on Zen 2-based products could allow an attacker to corrupt instructions executed at a different privilege level, potentially resulting in privilege escalation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Improper isolation of shared CPU resources in Zen 2-based products could allow attackers to corrupt instructions executed at different privilege levels, leading to potential privilege escalation and unauthorized access.
recommended_actions: Apply firmware or microcode updates provided by the CPU vendor to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict monitoring and logging of CPU activity to detect potential exploitation attempts.
Restrict access to systems running Zen 2-based CPUs and enforce least privilege principles for sensitive operations.

title: A DLL hijacking vulnerability in the AMD Cleanup Utility could allow an attacker to achieve...
description: A DLL hijacking vulnerability in the AMD Cleanup Utility could allow an attacker to achieve privilege escalation potentially resulting in arbitrary code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2024-36333.

title: A buffer overflow vulnerability within AMD Sensor Fusion Hub Driver can allow a local attacker to...
description: A buffer overflow vulnerability within AMD Sensor Fusion Hub Driver can allow a local attacker to write out of bounds, potentially resulting in denial of service or crash

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The buffer overflow vulnerability in the AMD Sensor Fusion Hub Driver could allow a local attacker to execute out-of-bounds writes, potentially causing a denial of service or system crash. This could disrupt operations and lead to downtime or data loss on affected systems.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or driver update from AMD addressing CVE-2025-29944 as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict local access to systems running the AMD Sensor Fusion Hub Driver to trusted personnel only.
Monitor systems for unusual behavior or crashes and implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: Insufficient parameter sanitization in TEE SOC Driver could allow an attacker to issue a...
description: Insufficient parameter sanitization in TEE SOC Driver could allow an attacker to issue a malformed DRV_SOC_CMD_ID_SRIOV_CHECK_TA_COMPAT to cause incorrect shared memory mapping, potentially resulting in unexpected behavior.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the TEE SOC Driver could lead to incorrect shared memory mapping, potentially causing unexpected behavior or system instability. While the severity is marked as low, exploitation could disrupt operations in systems relying on trusted execution environments.
recommended_actions: Apply patches or updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2025-66660.
Conduct a thorough review of system logs to identify any signs of exploitation or unusual activity.
Implement additional parameter validation checks in the driver to prevent malformed commands from being processed.

title: Use of uninitialized resource within the AMD Platform Management Framework (PMF) could allow an...
description: Use of uninitialized resource within the AMD Platform Management Framework (PMF) could allow an attacker to read a uninitialized kernel memory resulting in loss of confidentiality or availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the AMD Platform Management Framework (PMF) could allow an attacker to exploit uninitialized kernel memory, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data or system instability, resulting in loss of confidentiality or availability.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches or firmware updates provided by AMD to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict access controls and monitoring to detect and prevent unauthorized access to systems running AMD PMF.
Conduct a thorough review of systems and applications utilizing AMD PMF to identify potential exposure and ensure compliance with security best practices.

title: Rapid7 Metasploit Pro is vulnerable to a local privilege escalation attack that allows users to...
description: Rapid7 Metasploit Pro is vulnerable to a local privilege escalation attack that allows users to gain SYSTEM level control of a Windows host. Upon startup the metasploitPostgreSQL service the subsequent  postgres.exe service attempts to load an OpenSSL configuration file from a non-existent directory that is writable by standard users. By planting a crafted openssl.cnf file an attacker can trick the high-privilege service into executing arbitrary commands. This effectively permits an unprivileged

title: CVE-2026-41498 - Kimai is an open-source time tracking application. Prior to version 2.54.0, the Team API endpoints u
description: Kimai is an open-source time tracking application. Prior to version 2.54.0, the Team API endpoints use #[IsGranted('edit_team')] instead of #[IsGranted('edit', 'team')], causing Symfony TeamVoter to abstain from voting. This removes entity-level ownership checks on team operations, allowing any user with the edit_team permission to modify any team, not just teams they are authorized to manage. This issue has been patched in version 2.54.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41498.

title: CVE-2026-8128 - A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0. The affected element is an unkn
description: A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /admin/viewmsg.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument msgid results in sql injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to exploit an SQL injection flaw in the /admin/viewmsg.php file by manipulating the 'msgid' parameter. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, database compromise, or potential system takeover.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the 'msgid' parameter to prevent SQL injection attacks.
Update or patch the SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0 software if a fix is available from the vendor.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious SQL injection attempts.

title: CVE-2026-8127 - A vulnerability has been found in eladmin up to 2.7. Impacted is the function checkLevel of the file
description: A vulnerability has been found in eladmin up to 2.7. Impacted is the function checkLevel of the file /rest/UserController.java of the component Users API Endpoint. Such manipulation leads to improper access controls. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in eladmin up to version 2.7 allows improper access controls via the Users API Endpoint, potentially enabling unauthorized access to sensitive user data or administrative functions. The exploit is publicly disclosed and can be executed remotely, increasing the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade eladmin to a patched version if available, or apply temporary mitigations such as disabling the affected endpoint.
Implement strict access controls and monitor API usage for unusual activity to detect potential exploitation attempts.
Engage with the project maintainers to confirm a timeline for a fix or consider migrating to an alternative solution if no response is received.

This manipulation of the argument Name causes sql injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been published and may be used.

title: CVE-2026-6737 - An Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control vulnerability in AsusPTPFilter allows a local user
description:
An Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control vulnerability in AsusPTPFilter allows a local user to bypass driver security mechanisms and obtain restricted touchpad information or render the touchpad unusable via crafted IOCTL requests.Refer to the '
Security Update for ASUS Precision Touchpad ' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

description:
An Out-of-bounds Read vulnerability in the IOCTL handler in ASUS System Control Interface allows a local user to cause system crash (BSOD) via a read size that exceeds the buffer size.Refer to the '
Security Update for MyASUS ' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

title: CVE-2026-8124 - A security vulnerability has been detected in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects the function sidx_box
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects the function sidx_box_read of the file src/isomedia/box_code_base.c. The manipulation leads to allocation of resources. The attack must be carried out locally. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The identifier of the patch is 442e2299530138d8f874fd885c565ba98a6318ba. It is suggested to install a patch to address this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8124.

description: A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This impacts the function ogs_sbi_discovery_option_add_snssais in the library /lib/sbi/message.c of the component NSSF.
